title: SpaceX IPO closes up 19% and delivers the world's first trillionaire
why_matters: The world's first trillionaire company was built on rockets and satellites — hard physical infrastructure — signaling that patient capital in deep-tech manufacturing can reach software-scale returns.

title: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raises $12B to build an 'artificial general engineer' for the physical world
why_matters: Physical AI — intelligence applied to design and manufacturing, not just software — opens a new problem class; $12B in a single raise signals investors believe physical-world moats are now as compelling as software ones.

title: Why "reprogramming" is the buzziest approach to reversing aging right now
why_matters: If reprogramming works in humans, it redefines healthcare from treating disease to biological rollback — a foundational platform, not just a drug, with enormous buildable territory above it.

title: Google DeepMind is worried about what happens when millions of agents start to interact
why_matters: When you build products with agents, the agents don't just interact with your users — they interact with other agents; understanding emergent risks now is a prerequisite for building safely at scale.

title: Mistral is rumored to be raising €3B at €20B valuation
why_matters: A near-doubling of Mistral's valuation in nine months signals that sovereign AI — governments and enterprises preferring locally controlled models — is becoming a real procurement category, not just political rhetoric.
